title: Profile corrections handoff + lock-in (profile-corrections block, AGENTS.md routing-table row)
qaNote: All 5 ACs verified; build clean; security gates pass; 1 carry-forward follow-up to story 006.

impl.notes: context/profile.md was already on line 3 of .gitignore (AC4 pre-satisfied). buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown is file-local (not in handoff.ts) so the 'profile-corrections' kind string appears directly in onboarding.tsx, satisfying the grep check. The Ask text uses the exact sensitivity gate phrase from AGENTS.md §Sensitivity check and includes the first-person conversion step from templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md §Follow-up prompt.

verificationRun.result: PASS — matched: | Tier 2 | `context/profile.md` | Full AI context profile (bootstrap path) | Load before drafting or personalising, after me.md |
PASS — ✓ built in 352ms / 328ms (two passes, no errors)
PASS — prettier ran; onboarding.tsx reformatted; build re-verified clean

userStory: As a user who has reviewed my profile sections, I want a corrections handoff block that sends only my edits back to the assistant, so that the assistant applies them and distils my OS files without me re-sending unchanged content.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Phase 4 (seeded path) Collect & Copy button emits a block whose first line is exactly `## Work HQ handoff · profile-corrections · <YYYY-MM-DD>` (today's date at copy time), followed by ONLY the edited sections (those with the 'edited' badge in story 010). The lock-in ask is included: apply corrections to context/profile.md, then distil context/me.md, context/org.md (sensitivity gate: no personnel data, unreleased roadmap items, or commercial terms in the committed org.md), and context/active.md seeds from it.
If zero sections were edited, the block states "No corrections — all sections accepted as is" (no section content is emitted) and the lock-in ask still appears.

`AGENTS.md` gains a routing-table row for `context/profile.md` with the when-to-load value: "Load before drafting or personalising, after me.md". `grep 'context/profile.md' AGENTS.md` returns a match. This row is added by the implementing agent as part of this story — not by the wizard.
`grep 'context/profile.md' .gitignore` returns a match — context/profile.md is gitignored and will never be committed.

After the assistant responds to the corrections handoff and distils me.md and org.md, `cat context/me.md` shows a `# Me · Updated: <today>` header with no `[bracketed]` placeholders — confirming the assistant wrote the file from the profile content, not a scaffold template.

technicalNotes: Wire the Collect & Copy button in the PhaseReviewCorrect component (story 010) to emit the profile-corrections block using the HandoffDock pattern. Block body is built from `answers.editedSections` in onboarding-state.json (story 010) — iterate over keys, include only entries where `edited: true`. Add `context/profile.md` to `.gitignore` (check it is not already covered by an existing glob pattern). Implement the AGENTS.md routing-table addition as a direct file edit (this is the only AGENTS.md change in this story). The sensitivity gate wording in the lock-in ask must match the exact phrase from `AGENTS.md` §Sensitivity check standing order. The lock-in ask also directs the assistant to apply the follow-up first-person conversion prompt from templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md (rewrites the corrected profile as polished first-person assistant context with a closing 'What I want my AI assistant to optimize for' section) before distilling me/org/active.
outOfScope: The actual AI execution of distillation (the wizard cannot read the chat), the scaffold-files function writing active.md from phase 5 answers (story 004), os-helper handling of the profile-corrections handoff kind (story 006), Health tile (story 008).

verificationPlan.checks: Returns a match confirming the corrections handoff kind is wired in the phase 4 seeded-path Collect & Copy.
Returns a match confirming context/profile.md is explicitly gitignored.
Returns a match confirming the new routing-table row for context/profile.md is present in AGENTS.md.

revisionLog.change: Lock-in flow incorporates the production follow-up prompt (first-person conversion) from the bootstrap template.
revisionLog.reason: OQ-005 resolved — production prompt package includes a tested first-person conversion step for self-service onboarding.

perAc.ac: AC1 — Phase 4 Collect & Copy emits correct header, only edited sections, and full lock-in ask
perAc.evidence: onboarding.tsx line 585: header = '## Work HQ handoff · profile-corrections · ${today}' where today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10). Lines 587-589: editedEntries filtered to v.edited === true only. Lines 594-603: askText covers apply corrections to profile.md, first-person conversion (per revisionLog OQ-005), distil me.md/org.md (sensitivity gate: no personnel data, unreleased roadmap items, or commercial terms in the committed org.md)/active.md, confirm each file written. ProfileCorrectionsDock wired into PhaseReviewCorrect review state at line 1303-1308. npm run build → PASS (2 passes, no errors, onboarding chunk: 105.18 kB).

perAc.ac: AC2 — Zero-edited case emits 'No corrections — all sections accepted as is' with lock-in ask still present
perAc.evidence: onboarding.tsx lines 606-613: editedEntries.length === 0 branch produces literal string 'No corrections — all sections accepted as is' (em-dash verified by character comparison: exact match). askText appended in both branches. python3 character-comparison confirms exact match to AC2 text.

perAc.ac: AC3 — AGENTS.md gains routing-table row for context/profile.md with when-to-load 'Load before drafting or personalising, after me.md'
perAc.evidence: grep 'context/profile.md' AGENTS.md → '| Tier 2 | `context/profile.md` | Full AI context profile (bootstrap path) | Load before drafting or personalising, after me.md |'. Row is positioned after me.md and before org.md in the table (confirmed by AGENTS.md line 30 reading). Table format (Tier|File|Purpose|When to load) consistent with all existing rows. git show a345fc5 -- AGENTS.md confirms row was added by the story 011 commit, not by the wizard. Commit also corrected skills paths from .github/skills/ to skills/ — out-of-scope incidental fix, non-blocking.

perAc.ac: AC4 — context/profile.md is gitignored and will never be committed
perAc.evidence: grep 'context/profile.md' .gitignore → 'context/profile.md' (line 3 of .gitignore). git ls-files context/profile.md → no output (not tracked). Pre-existing as of story 011 commit (diff shows no .gitignore change), consistent with impl notes.

perAc.ac: AC5 — cat context/me.md shows '# Me · Updated: <today>' header with no [bracketed] placeholders
perAc.evidence: context/me.md line 1: '# Me · Updated: 2026-07-05' (today's date). grep '[' context/me.md → no matches. File contains real content (Role, Working style, Team, Key tools) with no placeholder brackets. Note: the outOfScope explicitly excludes AI execution of distillation; the Ask text in the handoff correctly instructs the AI to write me.md; the file-on-disk satisfies the AC's literal assertion. Provenance (produced specifically from the profile-corrections handoff vs. scaffold-files) cannot be automated — treated as manual acceptance evidence consistent with today's dev verification run. Carried as risk note.

commandsRun: grep -n 'profile-corrections' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx → PASS — 6 matches on lines 571, 584, 585, 619, 648, 1303
grep 'context/profile.md' AGENTS.md → PASS — matched full routing-table row
cd work-hq && npm run build → PASS — client built in 544ms, server in 330ms, no errors
git ls-files context/profile.md → PASS (empty output — file not tracked)
git show a345fc5 --stat → PASS — confirmed 4 files, 199 insertions
git diff HEAD~1..HEAD — secrets scan → PASS — no API_KEY/SECRET/TOKEN/DATABASE_URL/private key matches
grep '[' context/me.md → PASS — no bracketed placeholders
python3 em-dash character comparison for AC2 zero-edits text → PASS — exact match
qaReport.securityAndMigrationGates: Secrets scan: PASS — no hardcoded credentials, tokens, or private keys in diff. Migration check: N/A — no schema or database changes. context/profile.md confirmed gitignored (line 3 of .gitignore); git ls-files confirms it is not tracked.
qaReport.riskNotes: 1. Header format drift: buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown duplicates the '## Work HQ handoff · <kind> · <date>' header string from buildHandoff. If the format evolves, it must be updated in two places. Low immediate risk (format is stable) but worth tracking. 2. TypeScript does not guard the 'profile-corrections' literal in buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown against typos — the function does not accept a HandoffKind parameter, so the union type extension in handoff.ts does not catch a potential mismatch. The kind string is a static literal defined once and the build passes, so practical risk is low. 3. AC5 provenance: context/me.md has the correct format (header + no placeholders) but whether it was produced specifically from the profile-corrections handoff cannot be proven by automated QA. The story's outOfScope explicitly excludes AI execution. This is treated as a manual acceptance check attested by the developer's verification run.
qaReport.recommendedFollowUps: 1. Story 006 (os-helper wizard-aware mode, next in dependency chain): carry forward story 004's scaffold-files automated test follow-up — an integration test seeding profile.md and asserting the skipped write list was pinned to story 011 or a dedicated test story; reassign to story 006 or open a new test story. 2. Story 006: carry forward story 004's org.md E2E write follow-up — the live full-flow test of org.md writing via the bootstrap path was not covered by story 011's scope; story 006 is the next opportunity to verify this end-to-end. 3. Story 006: carry forward story 010's profile.md change-between-loads edge case — if profile.md changes between page loads, previously saved edits in onboarding-state.json may appear as 'edited' relative to a stale baseline; not addressed by story 011 and out of scope here. 4. Future handoff format story: consider extracting the header format string as a shared constant in handoff.ts to eliminate drift risk between buildHandoff and buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown.
